title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: Bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh herbs and lemon wedges.
seo.imageCaption: Warm and comforting, this authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) is a staple in Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: This hearty Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a comforting and nutritious staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. Bursting with earthy lentil flavor, brightened by lemon and fresh herbs, it offers a warm embrace in every spoonful. It's a simple yet profoundly satisfying dish, perfect for a wholesome family meal.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and diced carrots, cooking for another 3 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Lentils
instructions.text: Stir in the ground cumin and turmeric powder, cooking for 1 minute until aromatic. Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the aromatics and spices.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth (or water). Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down.

instructions.title: Season and Blend (Optional)
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. For a smoother consistency, you can use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup. If you prefer a chunkier soup, skip this step.

instructions.title: Finish with Lemon
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice. The lemon is crucial for brightening the flavor of the soup and is a signature element of Lebanese Adas.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with a side of pita bread or a fresh salad.

tips: Always rinse your red lentils thoroughly before cooking to remove any dust or debris and help prevent foaming.
For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a pinch of dried mint or a bay leaf during the simmering process, removing the bay leaf before serving.
If the soup becomes too thick upon standing, simply add a splash of hot water or vegetable broth to reach your desired consistency when reheating.
article:
Embrace the comforting embrace of a bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known affectionately as Adas. This humble yet incredibly flavorful dish is a cornerstone of Middle Eastern cuisine, cherished for its nourishing qualities, ease of preparation, and deeply satisfying taste. Perfect for a chilly evening, a wholesome lunch, or as a restorative meal, Adas transcends mere sustenance, offering a taste of tradition and warmth in every spoonful. If you’re searching for a hearty, healthy, and genuinely authentic soup that captivates the senses, look no further than this beloved Lebanese classic.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, or Adas, is a simple yet profoundly delicious soup that holds a special place in the hearts and homes across the Middle East. Unlike some other lentil soups that might be chunky or complex, Adas is typically characterized by its smooth, velvety texture and vibrant, often golden-orange hue. It’s a testament to how a few basic ingredients, when combined thoughtfully, can create something truly extraordinary.

This soup is celebrated for being both incredibly nutritious and incredibly budget-friendly, making it a staple in everyday meals and particularly popular during religious fasting periods like Lent (from which it gets its name in some dialects). The star of the show, red lentils, cook down quickly into a creamy consistency, absorbing the aromatic flavors of sautéed onions, garlic, and earthy spices like cumin and turmeric. A generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the end is non-negotiable, providing the bright, zesty counterpoint that elevates the entire dish and makes Lebanese Red Lentil Soup so distinctively refreshing. It’s a shining example of the simple elegance found within Middle Eastern Soups and Stews.

## Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

Creating an authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup requires a few key ingredients, most of which are likely already in your pantry. The beauty of Adas lies in its simplicity and the quality of its fresh components.

*   **Red Lentils:** The foundation of the soup. Use split red lentils, as they cook faster and break down more easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture.
*   **Olive Oil:** A good quality extra virgin olive oil is essential for sautéing and for drizzling over the finished soup.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic base. Finely chopped yellow onion and minced garlic provide depth and flavor.
*   **Carrots:** Adds a touch of sweetness and enhances the soup's beautiful color.
*   **Potatoes (Optional but Recommended):** A small potato, peeled and diced, adds body and a lovely creaminess without needing actual cream.
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** The liquid base. Broth adds more flavor, but water works perfectly fine, especially if you plan to season generously.
*   **Spices:**
    *   **Cumin:** The most crucial spice, lending its warm, earthy aroma.
    *   **Turmeric:** Contributes to the vibrant color and offers a subtle, earthy undertone.
    *   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice:** Absolutely vital for the soup's signature bright and tangy finish. Do not skip this!
*   **Fresh Cilantro or Parsley:** For garnish, adding freshness and a pop of color.

## How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step

Preparing Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl of Adas:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium yellow onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Vegetables & Spices:** Stir in 1-2 diced carrots and 1 small diced potato (if using). Sauté for about 5 minutes, allowing them to soften slightly. Add 1 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1/2 teaspoon of ground turmeric, 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. Stir well, toasting the spices for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
4.  **Combine with Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to combine with the vegetables and spices. Pour in 6-7 cups of vegetable broth or water.
5.  **Simmer:** Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-30 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and the vegetables are soft. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend to Desired Consistency:** Once cooked, you have a choice. For a chunky soup, you can serve it as is. For the traditional creamy texture of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, use an immersion blender directly in the pot to blend the soup until smooth, or carefully transfer batches to a regular blender (vent the lid and cover with a towel to prevent pressure buildup) and blend until creamy.
7.  **Finish and Season:** Return the blended soup to the stove (if transferred). Stir in the juice of 1-2 fresh lemons, to taste. Taste the soup and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed. The lemon is key to the soup's bright flavor, so don't be shy!
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a swirl of extra virgin olive oil and garnish with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley.

## Tips for the Best Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

Achieving the perfect bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is easy with a few simple tricks:

*   **Don't Skip the Rinse:** Rinsing the lentils thoroughly removes starches that can make the soup cloudy or gummy.
*   **Bloom Your Spices:** Briefly toasting the cumin and turmeric with the aromatics helps to release their full flavor and aroma, deepening the soup's overall taste.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If your soup is too thick after blending, simply add a bit more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. If it's too thin, let it simmer uncovered for a few more minutes to reduce.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Bottled lemon juice simply doesn't compare to the vibrant, fresh tang of freshly squeezed lemon. Add it at the very end to preserve its brightness.
*   **Good Olive Oil Finish:** A final drizzle of high-quality extra virgin olive oil just before serving adds richness, sheen, and a wonderful peppery note.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Seasoning is personal. Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and especially lemon juice to your preference. Some prefer a tangier soup, others milder.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a wonderfully versatile dish that can be enjoyed in various ways, whether as a light main course or a hearty starter.

*   **Warm Pita Bread:** The classic accompaniment. Tear off pieces of warm pita to scoop up the creamy soup. Toasted pita or pita chips also add a delightful crunch.
*   **Fresh Green Salad:** A simple side salad with a light lemon-tahini dressing provides a refreshing contrast to the soup's richness.
*   **Olives & Pickles:** A small dish of briny olives and tangy Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) makes an excellent side, cutting through the richness and awakening the palate.
*   **Toppings:** Elevate your bowl with various toppings. Consider a sprinkle of Aleppo pepper for a mild warmth, a swirl of chili oil for a spicy kick, or even a dollop of plain yogurt (if not strictly vegan) for added creaminess. Crispy fried onions or fresh mint leaves also make fantastic garnishes.
*   **As a Mezze Item:** Serve smaller portions as part of a larger mezze spread, alongside hummus, baba ghanoush, and other small plates.
*   **Beverages:** Pair with a refreshing glass of water infused with mint and lemon, or a warm cup of herbal tea.

## Variations & Dietary Adaptations

One of the great things about Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is how adaptable it is, making it suitable for a range of dietary needs and personal preferences.

*   **Vegan & Gluten-Free:** Adas is naturally vegan and gluten-free, provided you use vegetable broth (not chicken broth) and ensure any accompanying bread is also gluten-free. This makes it an excellent choice for those with specific dietary restrictions.
*   **Add More Vegetables:** Feel free to incorporate other quick-cooking vegetables. Diced zucchini, spinach (stirred in at the very end until wilted), or even finely chopped celery can add extra nutrients and texture.
*   **Spice It Up:** For those who enjoy a bit of heat, a pinch of cayenne pepper or red pepper flakes can be added along with the other spices.
*   **Herbal Infusions:** While cilantro and parsley are common garnishes, some variations incorporate dried mint into the soup itself, adding a unique aromatic depth. Add a teaspoon of dried mint along with the other spices.
*   **Tomato Paste for Depth:** A tablespoon of tomato paste, sautéed briefly with the onions and garlic, can add a subtle richness and a deeper red hue to the soup.
*   **Heartier Version:** For a more substantial meal, you can stir in cooked short-grain rice or fine bulgur during the last 10 minutes of simmering. This will thicken the soup further and add more body.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky dimension, though it's not traditional.

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to experience the warmth, comfort, and rich culinary heritage of the Middle East. Simple, wholesome, and bursting with flavor, this Adas recipe promises to become a beloved staple in your kitchen, offering a delicious and nourishing meal that truly satisfies the soul. Enjoy every spoonful of this timeless classic!
